title: assistant-onboard Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-onboard skill's systematic codebase learning workflow.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-onboard skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against surface scan, architecture map, pattern recognition, output contract, and gap handling requirements.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: New repo onboarding produces orientation
cases.purpose: Checks that a new repository onboarding request performs surface scan, architecture mapping, pattern recognition, and orientation output.
cases.prompt: Get familiar with this repo before we start changing anything.
setup_context: The assistant-onboard skill instructions are active.
The current project has not been onboarded in this session.
No focus_area or depth was specified.
expected_behavior: Requires or infers project_path and defaults depth to standard when unspecified.
Prints onboarding progress steps for Surface scan, Architecture mapping, Pattern recognition, Identifying unknowns, and Generating project orientation.
Reads README, agent instructions, gitignore, root listing, build files, and CI/CD config before deeper mapping.
Samples representative files for conventions instead of reading every file.
Returns project_summary, key_files, conventions, artifacts, gaps, and status.
Uses project-local orientation as an artifact and treats durable cross-session memory as knowledge graph backed.
pass_criteria: The response includes the required onboarding phases.
The response outputs key_files with path and purpose.
The response outputs conventions with pattern and example.
The response lists specific gaps or questions.
fail_signals: Starts editing project files before onboarding.
Claims to read every file as the default approach.
Asks generic questions that are not tied to discovered gaps.

cases.title: Incremental onboarding refreshes context
cases.purpose: Checks that returning to a known project reads existing orientation, checks recent changes, updates rather than overwrites, and reports specific unknowns.
cases.prompt: Refresh your project context; this repo changed since our last session.
setup_context: The assistant-onboard skill instructions are active.
A project-local orientation artifact already exists.
The user asks for an incremental refresh rather than first-time onboarding.
expected_behavior: Reads the existing project orientation artifact if present.
Checks git history since the last session date or a reasonable refresh boundary.
Updates changed sections instead of regenerating or overwriting the full orientation blindly.
Reports how many changes or areas were refreshed.
Records artifacts as updated or none, depending on whether files changed.
Lists specific unknowns and questions tied to the changed areas.
pass_criteria: The response identifies this as incremental onboarding.
The response reads existing orientation before updating.
The response checks recent git changes.
The response says update, not overwrite.
The response reports specific gaps or questions.
fail_signals: Regenerates the orientation from scratch without reading the existing artifact.
Overwrites memory without preserving existing sections.
Reports no unknowns without inspecting changed areas.
